verticalAlphaResearch.upstreamDocsSource: FMP stable REST API developer docs snapshot

alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: /stable/earnings (actual vs estimated EPS/revenue + surpriseDelta) and /stable/earnings-calendar (forward report dates)
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Free LLMs only know stale, widely-reported beats from training data. FMP returns the structured per-quarter surprise delta (actual - estimated) and the forward earnings-calendar dates that no model can compute without a live filing feed — essential for catalyst timing.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: What is NVDA's earnings surprise delta over the last 8 quarters?

alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: /stable/insider-trading/search and /stable/insider-trading/latest (Form 4 filer, transactionType, securities, value) plus /stable/house-trades and /stable/senate-trades (politician, chamber, amount range)
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Form 4 insider filings and congressional trade disclosures are filed continuously and are not in any model's training context. FMP structures the filer, buy/sell type, share count, and reported value so a buyer can compute net insider flow and politician-ticker overlap that a free LLM simply cannot see.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Are insiders and senators net buying or selling XOM and META?

alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: DCF fair value, EV/EBITDA, and FMP's peer set are live computed values over current financials — a free LLM hallucinates these from memory and has no notion of today's stock price vs intrinsic value. The contributor composites DCF + EV + market cap + price change + peers into one relative-value frame.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Rank NVDA and its FMP peers by DCF upside vs current price.

alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Sector and industry rotation is intraday/daily live data. Free LLMs have no idea which of the 11 sectors is leading this month or what the Technology-sector average P/E is right now. FMP returns performance and P/E for every sector and industry in a single composite call.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Which sectors are outperforming YTD and what is the Technology-sector P/E?

alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: /stable/revenue-geographic-segmentation and /stable/revenue-product-segmentation (segment revenue by period) plus /stable/financial-growth (growthRevenue, growthEBITDA, growthFreeCashFlow, growthEPS series)
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Geographic and product-line revenue mix and multi-year growth-rate series are buried in 10-K footnotes. Free LLMs guess AAPL's Services vs Products split from memory; FMP returns the reported segment dollars and the YoY growth rates by line item directly.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Chart AAPL's revenue by geography and product line and AMZN's EBITDA growth trend.

alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: /stable/shares-float (freeFloat, floatShares, outstandingShares) plus the CIK -> /stable/institutional-ownership/dates -> /stable/institutional-ownership/extract-analytics/holder chain (top 13F holders, shares, value, pctHeld)
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Top 13F institutional holders require resolving CIK, picking the latest filing period, and extracting the holder table — a multi-step lookup no free LLM can perform. FMP collapses the whole chain into one call returning float plus ranked holders.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Who are the top 13F holders of JPM and what is the free float?

alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: /stable/economic-calendar (event, date, country, importance over a date range) and /stable/treasury-rates (yields across maturities)
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: The forward economic calendar (next FOMC, CPI, NFP dates) and the live Treasury yield curve are not in any model's training data. FMP returns both in one composite call so a buyer can see catalysts and the rate backdrop together.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: What FOMC/CPI events are scheduled in the next 14 days and what is the 10Y yield?

alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: /stable/financial-scores (altmanZScore, piotroskiScore, beneishMScore, workingCapital) and /stable/ratings-snapshot (FMP rating grade)
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Altman Z, Piotroski F, and Beneish M are computed from current financials — free LLMs cannot recompute them on demand and routinely state stale or hallucinated scores. FMP returns the live computed scores plus the FMP rating in one call.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: What is LLY's Altman Z and Piotroski score, and does it signal distress?

alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: /stable/sec-filings-search/symbol (formType, filingDate, link, finalLink) and /stable/sec-filings-8k (latest material-event feed) plus /stable/dividends (date, dividend, record/payment/declaration dates) and /stable/dividends-calendar (next ex-div dates)
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Recent 8-K material events and ex-dividend calendar dates are live filing/feed data. Free LLMs miss same-week 8-Ks and cannot produce the structured ex-div/record/payment date triple FMP returns per dividend.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: What 8-Ks and 10-Ks did COST file in the last 120 days and when is JNJ's next ex-div date?

alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: /stable/sp500-constituent, /stable/nasdaq-constituent, and /stable/dowjones-constituent (symbol, name, sector, subSector per member)
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Index membership changes (adds/drops) happen continuously and are not in training data. Free LLMs cannot reliably list the current S&P 500 or filter it by sub-industry; FMP returns the live constituent roster with sector tags.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Which S&P 500 constituents are in the Semiconductors industry?

crossToolOpportunities: Relative-value screen: get_peers(NVDA) -> get_valuation per peer (DCF upside + EV/EBITDA) -> get_growth per peer (revenue/EBITDA growth) -> rank the group cheapest-to-richest on DCF upside adjusted for growth, producing a buy/sell/hold table a free LLM cannot assemble.

Catalyst-sentiment convergence: get_earnings(surprises, TSLA) -> get_insider_activity(TSLA) -> get_congressional_trades(TSLA) -> overlay earnings surprise sign, net insider flows, and politician trade direction into one bullish/bearish/diverging signal on a single ticker.

Ownership-quality-valuation thesis: get_ownership(AAPL) -> get_quality_scores(AAPL) -> get_valuation(AAPL) -> test whether institutional accumulation is justified by Altman Z / Piotroski quality and DCF fair value vs price in one pass.

Sector-rotation drill-down: get_sector_performance (which sector leads YTD) -> get_index_constituents or screen_stocks (filter within that sector by market cap) -> get_valuation on the bellwether name -> confirm the rotation is valuation-supported rather than momentum-only.
upstreamUIGaps: The FMP web UI shows sector performance and sector P/E on separate pages; the contributor's get_sector_performance returns performance AND P/E for all 11 sectors and their industries in a single composite call, enabling direct rotation-vs-valuation comparison in one view.

Finding top 13F holders in the FMP UI requires manually looking up a company's CIK, navigating to the institutional-ownership page, selecting a filing period, and reading the holder table; get_ownership auto-resolves CIK -> latest filing period -> ranked holders plus the float in one call.

The FMP UI splits DCF fair value, enterprise value, market cap, and price change across four separate tools; get_valuation composites all four so a buyer sees intrinsic value, EV multiples, size, and multi-period returns side by side.

The FMP UI surfaces the economic calendar and the Treasury yield curve on different pages; get_macro_calendar returns the forward macro calendar AND the current yield curve together, so a buyer can read catalysts against the rate backdrop in one request.

The FMP UI exposes earnings surprise history and the forward earnings calendar separately; get_earnings switches between surprises and calendar in one tool, letting a buyer move from 'did they beat?' to 'when do they report next?' without re-navigating.
